name: scenario_a09576_business_ethics_water_allocation_model_mesa_jus_mer
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q2 2026: 7 critical issues have surfaced over 72 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Business Ethics. The immediate incident is a water allocation model dispute. The trigger is that model outputs are being challenged, and shifting allocations now can trigger downstream public health and economic harms. The board has pre-approved up to $4.5M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as justice vs mercy: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a morning press cycle. Boundaries: Any policy change must be reversible within 30 minutes. If the vendor cannot provide logs within 2 hours, the plan must assume worst-case exposure. No one is denied service solely due to an automated flag; a human fallback must exist. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Incident Director Mateo Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Incident Director Mateo Ishikawa, coordinating the Business Ethics response at Mesa Business Ethics Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want a decision that is bounded, reversible, and measurable—so we can move quickly without losing the ability to stop.

agents.name: Community Advocate Arjun Bennett
agents.identity: I am Community Advocate Arjun Bennett, coordinating the Business Ethics response at Mesa Business Ethics Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with weekly subgroup error-rate disclosure, independent log capture, and an appeals channel usable without counsel; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Incident Director Mateo Ishikawa must choose an authorization approach for this Business Ethics decision. What does Incident Director Mateo Ishikawa do?
